message.content:
Репозиторий: /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP (монорепо: server/ Node+TS+Express+Mongoose+OpenAI; client/ React+Vite — это админка).
Ревью изменений ветки feat/knowledge-bank поверх базы 6431bf3..HEAD (2 коммита: админ-UI банка + переменный размер теста и качество вопросов).
Сначала прочитай диапазон диффа:  git -C /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP diff 6431bf3 HEAD -- <относящиеся пути>
Затем читай окружающий код, чтобы понять контекст. Ищи РЕАЛЬНЫЕ дефекты корректности и интеграции, а не стиль.
Контекст фич:
 - Бэкенд KC/банк (questionBank.service.ts, ktp.controller.ts, ktp.routes.ts): новый read-эндпоинт listItems вопросов банка (admin); size-параметр assembleNodeTest (разрешено 5/10/15/20), size подмешан в реюз-хэш; generateForCoverage переписан на циклы раундов + разброс difficulty; contentHashOf теперь хеширует текст+варианты+правильный ответ.
 - Test.model.ts: валидатор questions.length сменён с ===10 на диапазон 1..120.
 - test.routes.ts/test.controller.ts: /tests/node-bank принимает size; /generate(+guest) валидируют questionCount.
 - client: ktp.api.ts (+методы KC/банка), ktp.types.ts (+типы), components/ktp/TopicKnowledgeBank.tsx (новый), KtpCatalogPage.tsx (врезка per-topic), гейтинг по role==='admin'.
Возвращай только обоснованные находки с file/location/severity/detail/suggestedFix.

Дименсия: КОРРЕКТНОСТЬ КЛИЕНТА (client/src/components/ktp/TopicKnowledgeBank.tsx, api/ktp.api.ts, types/ktp.types.ts, pages/admin/KtpCatalogPage.tsx).
Проверь: React-корректность (state, отсутствие гонок при ленивой загрузке, повторные клики при isLoading), гейтинг role==='admin' (нет ли утечки admin-действий teacher'у, и совпадает ли с бэкенд-middleware — list/coverage teacher+admin, остальное admin), обработка ошибок (долгая генерация, 400 «нет источника»/«мало вопросов»), корректность путей API и форм ответов (data.data), типобезопасность BankItem vs реальный ответ listItems (lean-документ с _id/ObjectId — сериализация). Соответствие визуальных задизейбленных кнопок (TODO) и реального бэкенда.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Now let me examine the backend to verify the API contracts, routes, middleware gating, and the listItems serialization. Let me read the relevant backend files.

input.description: Show backend ktp routes and controller diff
Read full ktp routes file

content.content:
import { Router } from 'express';
import { body, param, query } from 'express-validator';
import { ktpController } from '../controllers/ktp.controller';
import { authenticate, isAdmin, isTeacherOrAdmin, asyncHandler, validate } from '../middlewares';

/**
 * KTP ROUTES
 * Справочник КТП по предмету. Эталон РЕДАКТИРУЕТ только admin; чтение доступно и учителю
 * (нужно для маппинга тем книг на КТП при декомпозиции).
 */
const router = Router();

router.use(authenticate);

router.get(
  '/:subjectId',
  isTeacherOrAdmin,
  [param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId')],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.getCatalog.bind(ktpController))
);

router.post(
  '/:subjectId',
  isAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    body('year').optional().isInt({ min: 2000, max: 2100 }).withMessage('year must be 2000..2100'),
    body('version').optional().isInt({ min: 1 }).withMessage('version must be >= 1')
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.upsertMeta.bind(ktpController))
);

router.post(
  '/:subjectId/topics',
  isAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    body('title').trim().isLength({ min: 1, max: 300 }).withMessage('title is required (1..300)'),
    body('description').optional().trim().isLength({ max: 8000 }),
    body('order').optional().isInt({ min: 0 }).withMessage('order must be a non-negative integer'),
    body('code').optional().trim().isLength({ max: 80 })
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.addTopic.bind(ktpController))
);

router.patch(
  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId',
  isAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId'),
    body('title').optional().trim().isLength({ min: 1, max: 300 }),
    body('description').optional().trim().isLength({ max: 8000 }),
    body('order').optional().isInt({ min: 0 }),
    body('code').optional().trim().isLength({ max: 80 })
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.updateTopic.bind(ktpController))
);

router.delete(
  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId',
  isAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId')
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.deleteTopic.bind(ktpController))
);

router.post(
  '/:subjectId/reorder',
  isAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    body('orderedTopicIds').isArray({ min: 1 }).withMessage('orderedTopicIds must be a non-empty array'),
    body('orderedTopicIds.*').isMongoId().withMessage('orderedTopicIds must contain valid ids')
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.reorderTopics.bind(ktpController))
);

router.post(
  '/:subjectId/import',
  isAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    body('topics').isArray({ min: 1 }).withMessage('topics must be a non-empty array'),
    body('topics.*.title').trim().isLength({ min: 1, max: 300 }).withMessage('each topic needs a title'),
    body('year').optional().isInt({ min: 2000, max: 2100 }),
    body('version').optional().isInt({ min: 1 }),
    body('replace').optional().isBoolean()
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.importTopics.bind(ktpController))
);

// ==================== Knowledge Components (подтемы темы КТП) ====================

router.get(
  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/components',
  isTeacherOrAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId')
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.listComponents.bind(ktpController))
);

router.post(
  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/components/propose',
  isAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId')
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.proposeComponents.bind(ktpController))
);

router.post(
  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/components',
  isAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId'),
    body('id').optional().isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid component id'),
    body('title').optional().trim().isLength({ min: 1, max: 300 }),
    body('description').optional().trim().isLength({ max: 2000 }),
    body('order').optional().isInt({ min: 0 }),
    body('status').optional().isIn(['proposed', 'confirmed'])
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.upsertComponent.bind(ktpController))
);

router.post(
  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/components/confirm',
  isAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId'),
    body('kcIds').isArray({ min: 1 }).withMessage('kcIds must be a non-empty array'),
    body('kcIds.*').isMongoId().withMessage('kcIds must contain valid ids')
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.confirmComponents.bind(ktpController))
);

router.post(
  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/components/reorder',
  isAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId'),
    body('orderedKcIds').isArray({ min: 1 }).withMessage('orderedKcIds must be a non-empty array'),
    body('orderedKcIds.*').isMongoId().withMessage('orderedKcIds must contain valid ids')
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.reorderComponents.bind(ktpController))
);

router.delete(
  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/components/:kcId',
  isAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId'),
    param('kcId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid component id')
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.deleteComponent.bind(ktpController))
);

// ==================== Question Bank (банк вопросов узла) ====================

router.get(
  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/bank/coverage',
  isTeacherOrAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId')
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.bankCoverage.bind(ktpController))
);

router.post(
  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/bank/generate',
  isAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId'),
    body('minPerKc').optional().isInt({ min: 1, max: 20 }),
    body('difficulty').optional().isInt({ min: 1, max: 5 })
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.bankGenerate.bind(ktpController))
);

// Просмотр сгенерированных вопросов (с правильными ответами) — только admin.
router.get(
  '/:subjectId/topics/:topicId/bank/items',
  isAdmin,
  [
    param('subjectId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid subjectId'),
    param('topicId').isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid КТП topicId'),
    query('kcId').optional().isMongoId().withMessage('Invalid kcId'),
    query('status').optional().isIn(['draft', 'active', 'retired'])
  ],
  validate,
  asyncHandler(ktpController.listBankItems.bind(ktpController))
);

// TODO Phase A-next: PATCH/POST retire/DELETE для /:subjectId/topics/:topicId/bank/items/:itemId

export default router;

content.text: This confirms a serialization concern. The backend `listItems` returns a lean document where `_id` is a `Types.ObjectId` and `subjectId` is also an ObjectId. After JSON serialization over HTTP, `_id` becomes a string (Mongo ObjectId serializes to a hex string via `.toString()`), so `it._id` as a React key works. Now let me verify the `IQuestion` type to confirm `questionType`, `options`, `correctOption`, `aiExplanation` field names match what the component reads, and check the auth store user type and the `getApiErrorMessage`/`success` wrapper.

content.text: Now I have a critical finding. The coverage `perKc` uses `kcId: k.id` and the component matches it against `k._id` in `activeByKc.get(k._id)`. I need to verify whether the `KnowledgeComponent` returned by `listComponents` exposes `_id` (the client type says `_id`) while the coverage `perKc.kcId` comes from `k.id`. Let me check the `knowledgeComponentService.getConfirmed` and `listComponents` controller to see the actual serialized field (`_id` vs `id`) so I can confirm if there's a key mismatch.

input.description: Inspect knowledgeComponent service list/getConfirmed mapping

input.command: grep -rn -A 12 "async listComponents\|async list\b\|toClient\|\.id\b\|_id" /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/knowledgeComponent.service.ts 2>/dev/null | head -60; echo "=== controller listComponents ==="; grep -rn -A 8 "async listComponents" /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/controllers/ktp.controller.ts
input.description: Find how KC id is serialized to client

content.text: `list` returns `IKnowledgeComponent[]` straight from the lean subdocs, so each KC has `_id` (ObjectId, serialized to string over JSON). Coverage's `perKc.kcId` comes from `String(k._id)`. So `activeByKc.get(k._id)` works as long as `k._id` serializes as a string. This is consistent. Let me verify `IKnowledgeComponent` has `_id` and confirm the `IKnowledgeComponent` shape vs the client `KnowledgeComponent` type (e.g., whether `status`/`order` are always present). content.text:
This is a key finding. The route group `/admin/ktp/:subjectId` is guarded by `PrivateRoute roles={['admin', 'teacher']}`, so **teachers can reach `KtpCatalogPage`**, which now unconditionally renders `TopicKnowledgeBank`. Inside `TopicKnowledgeBank`, the lazy `load()` calls `listComponents` and `bankCoverage` (both teacher+admin on backend — OK). But the **mutating buttons are gated by `canEdit = role==='admin'`**, which matches the backend `isAdmin`. So far so good for teacher gating.

However, there's a real issue: `TopicKnowledgeBank` calls `bankCoverage` for every topic eagerly when expanded, and the "Показать вопросы" / generate buttons are admin-only. But let me verify the critical mismatch: the **`bank/items` endpoint is `isAdmin`-only on backend** and **`canEdit`-gated on client** — consistent. Good.

Now let me focus on the React correctness issues. Let me re-read the component's `toggle`/`load` race and the `flash` setTimeout cleanup, plus check whether `KnowledgeComponentInput` uses `id` while the `upsertComponent` client passes `{ title, status }` (no id) — that's fine. Let me verify the `addManual` race and double-submit on the OK/Enter manual-add button (no isLoading guard), and the propose button while items list stale logic.

Now I have enough to confirm the React-correctness findings. Let me verify a few remaining items: (1) whether `proposeComponents` returns ALL kcs (replacing state) or only the proposed ones — important because `propose` does `setKcs(await proposeComponents(...))` which replaces the full list; (2) the `flash` setTimeout has no cleanup on unmount; (3) the manual-add Enter/OK has no in-flight guard (double submit). Let me check `proposeComponents` and `confirmComponents` controller return values to confirm they return the full KC list (consistency for `setKcs`).

input.command: grep -rn -A 6 "async propose\b\|async confirm\b\|async deleteComponent\|async upsert\b\|async remove\b" /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/knowledgeComponent.service.ts | grep -n "return\|sortKc\|\.save\|map\|filter" | head -30
input.description: Check return values of KC mutation service methods

input.command: grep -rn -A 14 "async confirm(" /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/knowledgeComponent.service.ts | head -20; echo "=== propose return ==="; grep -rn -A 30 "async propose(" /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/knowledgeComponent.service.ts | grep -n "return" | head
input.description: Check confirm and propose return shape

content.text:
All KC mutation methods return the full sorted KC list, so `setKcs(await ...)` correctly replaces state. The serialization of `_id` from these `doc.save()`-then-return paths is the in-memory subdocument (where `_id` is an ObjectId), serialized to a string over HTTP. Consistent with `BankCoverage.perKc.kcId` being `String(k._id)`. Good — no key mismatch.

Now let me verify one subtle issue: `proposeComponents` and other mutation methods return Mongoose subdocuments (not lean). When serialized to JSON via `res.json`, Mongoose subdocs include `_id` as a string and the virtuals. But `list()` returns lean subdocs from `topic.knowledgeComponents` where `topic` came from a non-lean `loadCatalogTopic` (it uses `KtpCatalog.findOne` without `.lean()`). So `list` returns hydrated subdocs too. Fine.

Let me confirm the `node-bank` test route to cross-check the `size` integration is not part of this client dimension (it's the test flow, separate). Let me now confirm one last React detail: whether the manual-add OK button and Enter both can double-fire, and verify the `flash` timer leak. Let me read the remaining component tail (items render + disabled TODO buttons already seen in diff). I have all I need.

Let me do a final check on whether `bankItems` is even reachable for a teacher who navigates: the route is admin-only on backend and `canEdit`-gated on client, but a teacher could still trigger `load()` which is fine. Let me verify there's no place where a teacher's `load()` would hit an admin-only endpoint.

input.description: List all API calls in component to map gating

content.text:
Gating is consistent: `load()`/`reloadCoverage()` only call the teacher+admin endpoints (`listComponents`, `bankCoverage`); all mutating/admin-only calls (`propose`, `confirm`, `delete`, `upsert`, `bankGenerate`, `bankItems`) are behind `canEdit && (...)` JSX gates. This matches backend middleware exactly.

I now have a complete picture. Let me compile the findings, focusing on real correctness/integration defects.

Key findings:
1. **`flash` setTimeout has no cleanup** — if the component unmounts (e.g., topic deleted, navigation) during the 3.5s window, `setMsg` fires on an unmounted component. Minor in React 18 (no warning, just a no-op), low severity.
2. **Re-click protection on manual-add (`addManual`)** — the OK button and Enter handler have NO in-flight guard (no `isLoading`/disabled state). Rapid double Enter/click can fire two `upsertComponent` POSTs creating duplicate KCs.
3. **`difficulty` state default 3 mismatch on NaN** — `Number(e.target.value) || 3` resets to 3 on empty, but `minPerKc` resets to 1; acceptable. Not a defect.
4. **No race guard on `toggle`/`load`** — `loaded` is only set true after success; consistent. But `open` toggles immediately while `load` is async — re-clicking toggle during load won't re-fire load (guarded by `!loaded`), but collapsing then re-expanding before load completes will trigger a second `load()` (because `loaded` still false). Mild double-fetch race.
5. **`BankItem` type uses `correctOption` (single) but backend `IQuestion` supports `correctOptions`/matching** — component only renders single-choice correctness via `opt === it.question.correctOption`. For non-single-choice question types in the bank, the "correct" highlight silently shows nothing. Integration correctness gap.

Let me verify finding 5 — whether the bank can contain non-single-choice questions (does `generateKcQuestions` produce only single_choice?).

input.description: Check what question types generateKcQuestions produces

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

input.command: grep -rn "generateKcQuestions" /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/*.ts; echo "=== search build prompt / single_choice ==="; grep -rln "generateKcQuestions\|single_choice" /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/
input.description: Locate generateKcQuestions implementation

content.text:
Confirmed: the bank generation produces only `single_choice` questions with `options` + `correctOption`. So finding 5 (non-single-choice rendering) is not a real defect for the current bank — it's defensively fine. I'll drop it as a defect since the bank only ever holds single_choice items. The `(it.question.options ?? [])` and `opt === correctOption` rendering is correct for all current bank content.

Now I have a precise, evidence-backed set of findings. Let me also double-check the `flash` unmount concern severity and whether there is a genuine double-fetch on collapse/re-expand race. The component is per-topic and topics can be deleted via `ConfirmDeleteBtn` on the parent, so unmount during flash/load is plausible.

Let me confirm the manual-add double-submit is real by re-reading the add UI handlers (Enter + OK button both call `void addManual()` with no disable).

Confirmed. The manual-add OK button and Enter both fire `addManual()` with no in-flight guard. Since `addManual` only closes the form (`setAddOpen(false)`) after the await resolves, a fast double-Enter (or Enter+OK click) before the POST resolves will send two `upsertComponent` requests. Because `upsert` creates a new KC when no `id` is provided and dedup is by normalized title only inside `propose` (not `upsert`), this can create duplicate confirmed KCs. Let me quickly verify `upsert` has no title-dedup.

Confirmed: `upsert` with no `id` always `list.push(...)` a new KC with no title-dedup. So a double-submit on manual-add reliably creates two duplicate confirmed KCs. This is a real correctness defect.

I have all the evidence needed. Let me compile the structured findings.

findings.title: Двойной сабмит ручного добавления KC создаёт дубликаты подтем
findings.detail: Кнопка OK (onClick={() => void addManual()}) и обработчик Enter (onKeyDown) вызывают addManual() без какой-либо защиты от повторного клика: нет ни isLoading-флага, ни disabled, а форма закрывается (setAddOpen(false)) ТОЛЬКО после завершения await. Быстрый двойной Enter или Enter+клик OK до резолва POST отправит два запроса upsertComponent с одинаковым title и без id. На бэкенде knowledgeComponentService.upsert при отсутствии id безусловно делает list.push(...) НОВОГО KC и не дедуплицирует по названию (в отличие от propose). Итог — две одинаковые confirmed-подтемы, которые далее попадают в покрытие и сборку теста. В отличие от остальных мутаций (propose/confirm/generate/showItems), здесь нет своего in-flight state.
findings.suggestedFix: Добавить локальный флаг addingManual (как proposing/confirming): в addManual выставлять его в try/finally, делать кнопку OK disabled={addingManual} и игнорировать Enter, пока addingManual. Либо синхронно закрывать форму/чистить ввод до await (оптимистично), чтобы повторный сабмит был невозможен.

findings.title: Повторный сворачивание/разворачивание во время первой загрузки вызывает двойной fetch
findings.detail: toggle() запускает load() при условии next && !loaded. Флаг loaded выставляется в true только ПОСЛЕ успешного ответа (внутри try, строка 61). Если пользователь развернул блок, затем свернул и снова развернул, пока первый load ещё in-flight, условие !loaded всё ещё истинно — стартует второй параллельный load(). Гонки данных нет (оба пишут одинаковый результат), но это лишняя пара запросов listComponents+bankCoverage на узел. Защита loading не используется в toggle. Также при ошибке первого load (loaded остаётся false) каждое повторное разворачивание будет ретраить — это, скорее, плюс, но двойной параллельный запрос всё равно возможен.
findings.suggestedFix: Гейтить запуск ещё и по loading: if (next && !loaded && !loading) void load();. Либо ставить временный in-flight-флаг в начале load и проверять его в toggle.

findings.title: setTimeout в flash не очищается при размонтировании — setState на размонтированном компоненте
findings.detail: flash() вызывает setTimeout(() => setMsg(null), 3500) без сохранения id таймера и без очистки в useEffect-cleanup. TopicKnowledgeBank рендерится per-topic внутри KtpCatalogPage, а тему можно удалить через ConfirmDeleteBtn родителя — тогда компонент размонтируется. Если в окне 3.5с после flash (например, сразу после 'Подтема удалена'/'Сгенерировано') узел/страница размонтируются, таймер сработает и вызовет setMsg на размонтированном компоненте. В React 18 это тихий no-op (без варнинга), но это утечка таймера и потенциальный источник проблем при будущем рефакторинге. Аналогично таймер не сбрасывается при частых flash подряд — предыдущий таймер продолжает жить и может погасить более новое сообщение раньше времени.
findings.suggestedFix: Хранить id таймера в useRef, в flash чистить предыдущий перед установкой нового, и добавить useEffect(() => () => clearTimeout(ref.current), []) для очистки при размонтировании.

findings.title: Долгая генерация: нет видимого прогресса/таймаута, только спиннер кнопки; покрытие может рассинхронизироваться при ошибке
findings.detail: bankGenerate дёргает ИИ и LLM-judge в несколько раундов (MAX_ROUNDS=3 на бэкенде) — запрос может идти десятки секунд. UI показывает только isLoading={generating} на кнопке; нет таймаута, нет промежуточного статуса, при сетевом обрыве сработает getApiErrorMessage. Но есть и тонкая некорректность: при УСПЕХЕ setCoverage(res.coverage) обновляет покрытие; при ОШИБКЕ (например, частичная генерация + таймаут axios) покрытие в UI остаётся старым, хотя на сервере вопросы могли успеть создаться — пользователь увидит устаревшее число до ручного reloadCoverage (который тут не вызывается). Также подсказка 'Генерация дёргает ИИ и может занять время' есть только в title кнопки.
findings.suggestedFix: В runMutation/generate при ошибке всё равно вызывать reloadCoverage() (молчаливый), чтобы отразить реально созданные вопросы. Опционально — показать inline-индикатор 'Идёт генерация, это может занять до минуты' вместо одного спиннера.

findings.location: BankItem.question (строки 78-92) и рендер вопросов в TopicKnowledgeBank (строки 326-345)
findings.title: BankItem.question моделирует только single_choice; типы шире реального ответа listItems (нет defensive-полноты, но и нет покрытия других типов)
findings.detail: BankItem.question типизирован как { questionType: string; options?; correctOption?; aiExplanation? }. Рендер опирается на opt === it.question.correctOption (single). Сейчас это корректно: aiService.generateKcQuestions генерирует строго single_choice (questionText/options=4/correctOption), и в банк попадают только они — то есть подсветка верного варианта работает для всего текущего содержимого банка. Однако questionType типизирован как string (а не литерал 'single_choice'), и при будущем добавлении multiple_choice/matching item'ов в банк UI молча перестанет подсвечивать правильный ответ (correctOption будет пуст, correctOptions/correctMatching не читаются). Это не баг сегодня, а скрытая хрупкость контракта типов между клиентом и server IQuestion.
findings.suggestedFix: Сузить questionType до литерала 'single_choice' (отражая реальный инвариант банка) ИЛИ, если планируются другие типы, добавить correctOptions?: string[] в BankItem.question и ветвление рендера. Минимум — оставить комментарий, что банк сейчас только single_choice.

findings.location: Гейтинг canEdit vs бэкенд-middleware (строки 35-36, все ktpApi-вызовы)
findings.title: Гейтинг admin-действий корректен и совпадает с бэкендом — подтверждение (не дефект), с одной оговоркой про teacher на странице
findings.detail: Проверено: маршрут /admin/ktp/:subjectId защищён PrivateRoute roles={['admin','teacher']} (router/index.tsx:96, 111) — то есть teacher МОЖЕТ открыть KtpCatalogPage и увидеть встроенный TopicKnowledgeBank. Read-вызовы при ленивой загрузке (listComponents, bankCoverage) — teacher+admin и на бэкенде (isTeacherOrAdmin), совпадает. Все мутации и просмотр ответов (proposeComponents, confirmComponents, deleteComponent, upsertComponent, bankGenerate, bankItems) на клиенте обёрнуты в canEdit (role==='admin'), а на бэкенде — isAdmin. Соответствие точное, утечки admin-действий teacher'у нет: teacher видит только список KC и покрытие read-only, кнопок правок/генерации/просмотра вопросов не получает. Это подтверждение корректности; единственный нюанс — задизейбленные TODO-кнопки Править/Ретайр/Удалить (disabled, cursor-not-allowed) и реально отсутствующие на бэкенде эндпоинты согласованы (контроллер/роуты содержат лишь TODO-комментарии, ручек нет), так что мёртвых действий, бьющих в 404, у пользователя нет.
findings.suggestedFix: Действий не требуется. Опционально: чтобы не грузить лишние данные для teacher, можно подтвердить продуктовое намерение показывать teacher'у покрытие банка read-only; если нет — скрывать весь блок при !canEdit.

content.content: Structured output provided successfully

content.text:
I completed the review of the client dimension. Summary of what I verified and found:

**Real defects (worth fixing):**

1. **HIGH — Double-submit on manual KC add** (`TopicKnowledgeBank.tsx`, `addManual` lines 125-134 + handlers 264-273). The OK button and Enter both call `void addManual()` with no in-flight guard, and the form only closes after the await resolves. Confirmed against backend `knowledgeComponentService.upsert`: with no `id` it unconditionally `list.push(...)` a new KC and does NOT dedup by title — so a fast double-Enter reliably creates duplicate confirmed KCs that then feed coverage and test assembly.

2. **LOW — Double-fetch race in `toggle`/`load`** (lines 47-67, 77-81). `loaded` flips only after success, and `toggle` doesn't check `loading`, so collapse→re-expand during the first in-flight load fires a second parallel `listComponents`+`bankCoverage`.

3. **LOW — `flash` setTimeout has no cleanup** (lines 41-44). Timer id isn't stored/cleared; on unmount (topic deleted via parent `ConfirmDeleteBtn`) or rapid successive flashes, `setMsg` fires on an unmounted component / earlier timer clobbers a newer message.

4. **LOW — Stale coverage on generate error** (`generate`, lines 136-147). On a partial-then-failed long generation, UI coverage stays stale because `reloadCoverage()` isn't called in the error path.

5. **LOW — `BankItem.question` type fragility** (`ktp.types.ts` 78-92 + render 326-345). Render assumes single-choice (`opt === correctOption`). Correct for the current bank (verified `aiService.generateKcQuestions` emits only `single_choice`), but `questionType: string` is wider than reality and would silently fail to highlight correct answers if other types ever enter the bank.

**Verified correct (not defects):**

- **Gating matches backend exactly.** Route `/admin/ktp/:subjectId` allows `['admin','teacher']`, so teachers reach the page; lazy `load()` only calls teacher+admin endpoints (`listComponents`, `bankCoverage` = `isTeacherOrAdmin`), and every mutating/items call is behind `canEdit` (`role==='admin'`) matching `isAdmin`. No admin-action leak to teachers.
- **API paths and `data.data` shapes** all match the backend `success()` wrapper and route definitions.
- **`_id` key correctness:** coverage `perKc.kcId` is `String(k._id)` and the component matches `activeByKc.get(k._id)` — consistent under JSON serialization.
- **Disabled TODO buttons** (Править/Ретайр/Удалить) align with the backend, which only has TODO comments and no implemented item-management endpoints — no dead actions hitting 404.
